session.name: MPI-64 RunPod Remote Engine — Batches #1-#3.1 done + user-verified (secret store, Comfy boundary adapter, Settings section + boot gate, picker polish)

goal.original: Implement MPI-64 RunPod Remote Engine: run ComfyUI generation on a user's RunPod Secure Cloud GPU from the Cubric Vision desktop app, via a Cubric-owned hidden Pod template + token-auth HTTP wrapper, network-volume persistence, app-managed lifecycle, GPU/PyTorch compatibility gates.
goal.summary: Wrapper proven live on a real A4000 Pod (prior session). This session: Batch #2 (Comfy boundary adapter — backend proxy routes/remoteProxy.js + renderer adapter js/services/remoteEngineClient.js + ComfyUIController.httpBase() seam; local mode byte-identical, USER-VERIFIED with a real local generation) and Batch #3/#3.1 (Settings RunPod section, secrets IPC client, remote boot gate + advisory pop-up, volume-aware DC/GPU pickers; USER-VERIFIED incl. key save+validate and live EUR-IS-1 availability). Next: small UI polish (dropdown meta truncation + GPU $/hr), then Batch #4 / Phase 3. NOTHING committed.

completed: Phase 1 — all 6 design decision docs + gpu-availability-and-datacenter.md
Phase 2 — wrapper full contract 24/24 local verify + LIVE end-to-end on Pod k8pr35yto3xy7n (A4000, EUR-IS-1); readiness item still PARTIAL (manifest version gate + app polling helper remain)
Parallel Batch #1 — main/secretsStore.js + routes/runpodRemote.js + routes/remoteEngine.js, wired in server.js/main.js; smoke-tested via npm start

Parallel Batch #2 — routes/remoteProxy.js (proxy /proxy/* + /remote/mode|ws-token|comfy/status + SSE intercept mounted BEFORE comfy routes) + js/services/remoteEngineClient.js + comfyController httpBase()/_ensureRemoteReady + commandExecutor view-URL swap; 19/19 mocked-router test; local generation user-verified byte-identical
Parallel Batch #3 — js/core/secretsClient.js + runpodConfig (storageKeys/storage/state) + MpiSettings RunPod section + js/shell.js _bootApp remote gate + advisory; user-verified

Batch #3.1 — GET /runpod/volumes + volume dropdown + DC 'your volume' marker + mismatch warning + stock-first N/A-labeled GPU meta; user-verified labels correct; removed dead ComfyUI API URL field + mpi_comfy_url (changelog note in docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md)
pending: RunPod Settings UI polish (NEXT, small): (1) MpiDropdown option meta truncated/invisible at Settings panel width — make stock+VRAM readable; (2) add GPU price-per-hour to picker meta (GraphQL gpuTypes price fields, e.g. securePrice — verify exact field names live)
Parallel Batch #4 — compatibility/manifest validation UI (consumes volume-manifest-schema.md)
Phase 2 leftover — /wrapper/manifest version-match gate vs APP_VERSION/COMFY_VERSION (needs manifest written to volume — Phase 3)
Phase 3 — volume init/repair, remote model download onto volume (start with ONE SDXL checkpoint, NOT WAN), models/check routing to wrapper, workflow bundle versioning

Phase 4 — Pod auto-create/start at comfy-start trigger + quit-stop lifecycle, input-asset transfer (media/latent upload intercepts), end-to-end remote generation (SDXL image first), crash watchdog verify
Phase 5 — hardening, global logger redaction hook, secret-hygiene audit, user-facing cost docs
Push UPDATED full-contract image: docker push ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.1.0 (was blocked by GHCR 502; original live-tested image is public)

next_action.description: Implement the 'RunPod Settings UI polish' plan item (brief before code): fix MpiDropdown option-meta truncation in the Settings panel so stock+VRAM are readable (check MpiDropdown.css ellipsis/max-width vs a Settings-scoped fix; wrapLabels prop exists), and add GPU price-per-hour to the picker meta — extend client.gpuTypes GraphQL query in routes/runpodRemote.js with price fields (likely securePrice; VERIFY exact field names against the live API with the user's key), pipe through /runpod/gpu-availability into MpiSettings gpuOptions meta. Verify with the user at default panel width and spot-check prices vs the RunPod console. Then proceed to Batch #4 (manifest validation UI) or Phase 3 per user choice.

key_decisions: Transport implemented per research/transport-topology.md: Express backend proxy holds the wrapper token; renderer base-URL seam is ComfyUIController.httpBase() (returns http://127.0.0.1:8188 verbatim in local mode → byte-identical) + js/services/remoteEngineClient.js (refresh per ensureServerRunning; wsUrl(clientId) renderer-direct WSS exception, token in memory only).

routes/remoteProxy.js owns backend remote-mode state ({active, podId}, set via POST /remote/mode; 422 if activating without podId) and MUST stay mounted BEFORE routes/comfy.js in server.js (its GET /comfy/events/stream intercept falls through with next() when inactive).

Settings 'ComfyUI API URL' field + mpi_comfy_url + getComfyUrl/setComfyUrl REMOVED (user decision after learning it was dead config never read by the app). Changelog entry parked in docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md — new convention, fold in at next /mpi-version-bump.

GPU availability semantics (verified vs live console screenshots): GraphQL gpuAvailability.available:true does NOT equal console availability; console badge = stockStatus where null renders N/A but such cards may still deploy. UI mirrors console: stock leads dropdown meta, labels High/Medium/Low/N/A, N/A sorted last, all available:true cards stay selectable.

Non-secret RunPod prefs = state.runpodConfig {enabled,podId,datacenter,gpuType,volumeId}, localStorage key mpi_runpod_config; API key is write-only from renderer via js/core/secretsClient.js (secrets:* IPC; setApiKey returns {ok, weakEncryption} → one-time warning at save time).

Boot gate: js/shell.js _bootApp — RunPod enabled → _initRemoteBoot (pushes /remote/mode when podId set, advisory pop-up via showError('RunPod Active', ...), skips local engine version/install gate via `else try` so the local path is textually untouched); ComfyUI auto-start skipped in remote mode until Phase 4 Pod start.

Scope splits kept: media/latent upload intercepts = Phase 4; models/check routing = Phase 3; Pod create/start lifecycle = Phase 4; js/shell/memoryOps.js VRAM unload deliberately stays hardcoded local.
constraints: NEVER commit unless the user explicitly asks. ALL MPI-64 app work is uncommitted on branch RunPod (only worktree: c:/AI/Mpi/Cubric-Vision).
User chose INLINE-SEQUENTIAL execution for app code — do not dispatch sub-agent workers for implementation.

RunPod secrets + standing resource IDs in GITIGNORED c:/AI/Mpi/cubric-remote-wrapper/.secrets/runpod.env: Pod k8pr35yto3xy7n (STOPPED, A4000, EUR-IS-1), volume cubric-vision-vol cfwqthmdrg (150GB, EUR-IS-1, ~$10.50/mo), template ejrwtsej15, image ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.1.0 (public). NEVER paste the key in chat/logs. User's key is also SAVED in the app's secret store now (safeStorage).

RunPod proxy + API are behind Cloudflare: always send a browser User-Agent (UA const exists in runpodRemote.js/remoteProxy.js/remoteEngine.js); Windows curl flakes on POST-multipart through the proxy — use Python for remote tests.
GraphQL key goes in the Authorization header, NEVER the URL (fixed key-leak); redactSecret() in runpodRemote.js scrubs rpa_/Bearer/api_key=.
routes/logger.js warn/info take only (category, message) — 3rd arg silently dropped; only error() takes err.

Settings file MpiSettings.js: _initFields re-runs on every panel open via el.onOpen; _runpodAvailability/_runpodVolumes cache per component instance; volume REST list shape handled defensively (array | .networkVolumes | .volumes).
state/index.json may carry a BOM (PowerShell-written) — read with utf-8-sig in Python scripts.
The frozen 1.0.0 tutorial mentions the removed ComfyUI API URL field — next release notes MUST call out the removal (entry already in docs/releases/UNRELEASED.md).
DOCS WEBSITE PUSH BLOCK still applies (never push c:/AI/Mpi/Cubric Studio (Docs)).

RunPod secrets + standing resources (Pod k8pr35yto3xy7n stopped, volume cfwqthmdrg, template ejrwtsej15, all EUR-IS-1) are in GITIGNORED c:/AI/Mpi/cubric-remote-wrapper/.secrets/runpod.env — never paste the key; it is also saved in the app's secret store.
